topPlaces.name: Sujru
topPlaces.description: Sujru, also known as Sujroo, is a village in the state of Uttar Pradesh. It is located on the outskirts of Muzaffarnagar, about 3 kilometres south of the city centre.

topPlaces.name: Lachhera
topPlaces.description: Lachhera (or Lacheda) is a village in Muzaffarnagar Tehsil of Muzaffarnagar District in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It belongs to Saharanpur Division.

topPlaces.name: 2013 Muzaffarnagar riots
topPlaces.description: The 2013 Muzaffarnagar riots were religious clashes between the Hindu Jats and Muslims in the Muzaffarnagar district of Uttar Pradesh, India. A total of 62 died in the clashes, 42 of whom were Muslims, and 20 Hindu.

topPlaces.name: Muzaffarnagar
topPlaces.description: Muzaffarnagar (Hindi pronunciation: [mʊzəfːəɾ.nəgəɾ], also [mʊd͡ʒəp.pʰəɾ.nəgəɾ]) is a city under Muzaffarnagar district in the Indian State of Uttar Pradesh. It is situated midway on the Delhi - Haridwar/Dehradun National Highway (NH 58) and is also well connected with the national railway network.

topPlaces.name: Ghasipura, Uttar Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Ghasipura (derived from 'Ghasi-', the first name of the founder, and 'pura-', the Sanskrit word for city, castle or fortress) is a village in the Muzaffarnagar district of Uttar Pradesh. It is located on NH 58 between Muzaffarnagar (13 km) and Meerut (44 km) and is almost equidistant from New Delhi (115 km) and Dehradun (133 km).

items.note: Sujru, also known as Sujroo, is a village in the state of Uttar Pradesh. It is located on the outskirts of Muzaffarnagar, about 3 kilometres south of the city centre.
Lachhera (or Lacheda) is a village in Muzaffarnagar Tehsil of Muzaffarnagar District in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It belongs to Saharanpur Division.

The 2013 Muzaffarnagar riots were religious clashes between the Hindu Jats and Muslims in the Muzaffarnagar district of Uttar Pradesh, India. A total of 62 died in the clashes, 42 of whom were Muslims, and 20 Hindu.

Muzaffarnagar (Hindi pronunciation: [mʊzəfːəɾ.nəgəɾ], also [mʊd͡ʒəp.pʰəɾ.nəgəɾ]) is a city under Muzaffarnagar district in the Indian State of Uttar Pradesh. It is situated midway on the Delhi - Haridwar/Dehradun National Highway (NH 58) and is also well connected with the national railway network.

Ghasipura (derived from 'Ghasi-', the first name of the founder, and 'pura-', the Sanskrit word for city, castle or fortress) is a village in the Muzaffarnagar district of Uttar Pradesh. It is located on NH 58 between Muzaffarnagar (13 km) and Meerut (44 km) and is almost equidistant from New Delhi (115 km) and Dehradun (133 km).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vahelna Jain temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vahelna Jain temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vahelna Jain temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dehradun Airport (Jolly Grant) (97 km) and the nearest railway station is Haridwar Junction (74 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vahelna Jain temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vahelna Jain temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vahelna Jain temple?
faq.a: Stays in Vahelna Jain temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vahelna Jain temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sujru, Lachhera, 2013 Muzaffarnagar riots, Muzaffarnagar, Ghasipura, Uttar Pradesh.
